title: Listening - Part 3: Learning a Musical Instrument
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they chose to learn a particular musical instrument. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for their choice. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about why they chose to learn a particular musical instrument. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for their choice. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about choosing a musical instrument.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: When I said I'd like to learn an instrument, I was considering the guitar because several friends played it. Before I bought one, my aunt mentioned an old violin stored in her attic. [19]It had belonged to my grandfather, and because she was happy to lend it to me, I chose violin rather than spend money on another instrument.[/19] I didn't expect it to be easy, and it certainly isn't, but using something that once mattered to my grandfather makes practising feel special.||Speaker 2 Man: I wanted an instrument I could take to gatherings with friends because we often play music together outdoors in summer. The piano obviously wasn't practical, and even carrying a guitar on the bus seemed awkward. [20]The ukulele appealed to me because it fits into a small case and I can bring it anywhere without struggling with heavy equipment.[/20] It only has four strings, although producing a good rhythm still takes practice. At least taking it to lessons is effortless.||Speaker 3 Woman: My parents kept suggesting piano lessons, saying it would be useful for understanding music. But the songs I listen to are mostly jazz recordings, and I always notice one particular instrument above the others. [21]I chose the saxophone because I love the solos played by my favourite musicians and wanted to learn how they produce that powerful sound myself.[/21] My first attempts were not pleasant for anyone in the house, but recently I’ve begun to play simple tunes properly.||Speaker 4 Man: Some friends at school formed a band and asked whether I wanted to join. They already had two guitarists, a drummer and a singer. I'd never considered the keyboard before; I actually preferred drums. [22]However, the band needed somebody to play keyboard parts in several songs, so I agreed to learn enough to fill the missing role.[/22] It began as a practical decision, but I've found I like being able to change the whole atmosphere of a song with a few chords.||Speaker 5 Woman: I live in a flat with thin walls, and my neighbour works night shifts, so I had to think carefully before starting music lessons. I would love to play trumpet, but practising that at home would be impossible. [23]I settled on a digital piano because I can plug headphones into it and practise in the evening without making any noise that disturbs anyone else.[/23] Having headphones also means I’m less embarrassed when I repeat the same difficult section twenty times.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: When I said I'd like to learn an instrument, I was considering the guitar because several friends played it. Before I bought one, my aunt mentioned an old violin stored in her attic. It had belonged to my grandfather, and because she was happy to lend it to me, I chose violin rather than spend money on another instrument. I didn't expect it to be easy, and it certainly isn't, but using something that once mattered to my grandfather makes practising feel special.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I wanted an instrument I could take to gatherings with friends because we often play music together outdoors in summer. The piano obviously wasn't practical, and even carrying a guitar on the bus seemed awkward. The ukulele appealed to me because it fits into a small case and I can bring it anywhere without struggling with heavy equipment. It only has four strings, although producing a good rhythm still takes practice. At least taking it to lessons is effortless.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My parents kept suggesting piano lessons, saying it would be useful for understanding music. But the songs I listen to are mostly jazz recordings, and I always notice one particular instrument above the others. I chose the saxophone because I love the solos played by my favourite musicians and wanted to learn how they produce that powerful sound myself. My first attempts were not pleasant for anyone in the house, but recently I’ve begun to play simple tunes properly.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: Some friends at school formed a band and asked whether I wanted to join. They already had two guitarists, a drummer and a singer. I'd never considered the keyboard before; I actually preferred drums. However, the band needed somebody to play keyboard parts in several songs, so I agreed to learn enough to fill the missing role. It began as a practical decision, but I've found I like being able to change the whole atmosphere of a song with a few chords.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I live in a flat with thin walls, and my neighbour works night shifts, so I had to think carefully before starting music lessons. I would love to play trumpet, but practising that at home would be impossible. I settled on a digital piano because I can plug headphones into it and practise in the evening without making any noise that disturbs anyone else. Having headphones also means I’m less embarrassed when I repeat the same difficult section twenty times.

options.text: A relative already had one available.
It was easy to carry around.
It featured in music they admired.
Their group needed someone to play it.
It would allow them to perform alone.
It was cheaper than other choices.
They could practise without disturbing people.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker chose violin because a family instrument was readily available to borrow.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The ukulele's small size and portability were the decisive factors.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker selected saxophone after admiring it in favourite jazz recordings.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker learned keyboard because it filled an unoccupied role in a school band.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The digital piano lets the speaker practise silently through headphones.
